explanation: The Sigma rule excludes any CommandLine containing the string '.sdb' (filter_main_legit_ext). By placing the malicious shim inside a directory that has '.sdb' in its name, the command line will contain '.sdb', effectively bypassing the detection while installing a file with a non-standard extension (.bin).

The rule explicitly filters out executions where the CommandLine ends with ' -c' (filter_main_legit_extensions). By appending the ' -c' flag to the end of the execution command, the malicious shim with an uncommon extension (.bin) evades detection.
